instruction: Send an email to a public park office asking about picnic reservations. The email must include the adjective "available" in the sense of "able to be used or obtained" and the verb "confirm" in the sense of "establish the truth or correctness of something." Begin the email with the phrase "Dear Park Office," and conclude it with the phrase "Looking forward to your response." The email should contain exactly 150 words, with each sentence containing no more than 20 words. Write the email exclusively in English, incorporating a relevant English idiom. Tailor the email for a general audience, avoiding technical jargon, and provide a brief explanation of why the reservation is needed, including the intended date, number of attendees, and any specific requirements. Do not disclose the number of attendees in the email.
response:
Dear Park Office,

I am writing to ask about picnic spot availability next Saturday. We hope to find a space that is available for our group. Our family plans a special gathering under the warm summer sun. We need to confirm the details before we finalize our travel plans. Please tell us if any large areas are open for booking soon. It is better to plan ahead than to miss this perfect chance. We require a flat surface with plenty of shade for everyone. Kindly confirm the exact time we must arrive to secure our spot. Your quick reply will help us avoid any last-minute stress or worry. We want to ensure everything runs smoothly for all our guests. Thank you for your time and help with this important request today. Looking forward to your response.

Conflicts between Content Constraints.new_constraint: Do not disclose the number of attendees in the email.
Conflicts between Content Constraints.org_constraint: The email must include the number of attendees.
